name: Shiki
what_it_does: Syntax highlighter using TextMate grammars and VS Code themes. Shiki produces HTML output with VS Code-accurate syntax highlighting — the same engine that powers VS Code's syntax coloring. Supports 100+ languages and all VS Code themes. v1.0 (Shikiji successor) added true ESM, WASM bundle for browser use, and dual-theme support for dark/light mode. Used in VitePress, Twoslash, and many documentation frameworks.
use_cases: Add VS Code-quality syntax highlighting to documentation sites, technical blogs, and developer tools
Generate highlighted code snippets for blog posts, READMEs, and documentation in agent content pipelines
Implement syntax highlighting in custom Markdown processors with shiki's remark/rehype plugins
Build code preview components in web applications with accurate multi-language highlighting
Support dual light/dark mode themes in documentation using Shiki's CSS variables mode
not_for: Real-time editor syntax highlighting — use CodeMirror or Monaco; Shiki is for static HTML output
Client-side highlighting of user-provided code at runtime — Shiki's WASM bundle is large (~900KB); use Prism.js for lightweight client-side highlighting
Minimal bundle size — Prism.js or highlight.js are lighter for simple use cases
best_when: You need VS Code-quality syntax highlighting in documentation, static sites, or pre-rendered HTML — Shiki's TextMate grammar support beats other highlighters for accuracy.
avoid_when: You need lightweight client-side highlighting (use Prism.js) or real-time editor functionality (use CodeMirror or Monaco).

auth.notes: No authentication — local code highlighting library.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Invalid language IDs throw descriptive errors listing available languages. Theme loading errors include theme name context. HTML output is deterministic.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Highlighting is deterministic — same code + same language + same theme always produces same HTML.
known_agent_gotchas: Shiki v1 (Shikiji) changed API significantly from v0.x — createHighlighter() replaces getHighlighter(); old tutorials and code don't work with v1
WASM initialization is async — must await createHighlighter() before calling highlight functions; synchronous usage patterns don't work
Bundle size is large (~1-5MB with grammars) — use lazy loading with codeToHtml() and only load languages/themes you need
Language grammars are loaded lazily in v1 — first highlight of a new language has extra latency as grammar is fetched; preload frequently-used languages
CSS variables mode (dual theme) requires additional CSS configuration to switch between light/dark modes
Unknown languages fall back to plain text — check language name spelling if highlighting appears plain; language IDs are case-sensitive

security_score_components.security_notes: Output HTML should be sanitized if rendering untrusted code — Shiki escapes most HTML but user-provided code may contain HTML injection if rendered without sanitization.
